2604.08062.title: From Gaze to Guidance: Interpreting and Adapting to Users' Cognitive Needs with Multimodal Gaze-Aware AI Assistants
2604.08062.paper_summary: Current LLM assistants are powerful at answering questions, but they have limited access to the behavioral context that reveals when and where a user is struggling. We present a gaze-grounded multimodal LLM assistant that uses egocentric video with gaze overlays to identify likely points of difficulty and target follow-up retrospective assistance. We instantiate this vision in a controlled study (n=36) comparing the gaze-aware AI assistant to a text-only LLM assistant. Compared to a conventional LLM assistant, the gaze-aware assistant was rated as significantly more accurate and personalized in its assessments of users' reading behavior and significantly improved people's ability to recall information. Users spoke significantly fewer words with the gaze-aware assistant, indicating more efficient interactions. Qualitative results underscored both perceived benefits in comprehension and challenges when interpretations of gaze behaviors were inaccurate. Our findings suggest that gaze-aware LLM assistants can reason about cognitive needs to improve cognitive outcomes of users.
2604.08062.paper_summary_zh: 目前的 LLM 助手在回答問題方面非常強大，但它們對用戶掙扎的行為背景的訪問有限。我們提出了一種以注視為基礎的多模態 LLM 助手，利用帶有注視覆蓋的自我中心視頻來識別可能的困難點並針對後續的回顧性幫助。我們在一項控制研究中實現了這一願景（n=36），比較了注視感知的 AI 助手與僅文本的 LLM 助手。與傳統的 LLM 助手相比，注視感知助手在對用戶的閱讀行為評估中被評價為顯著更準確和個性化，並顯著提高了人們回憶信息的能力。用戶在與注視感知助手交互時說的話顯著更少，這表明了更高效的互動。質性結果強調了在理解上的感知好處以及當注視行為的解釋不準確時所面臨的挑戰。我們的研究結果表明，注視感知的 LLM 助手可以推理認知需求，以改善用戶的認知成果。
2604.08062.author: Valdemar Danry et.al.
2604.08062.authors: Valdemar Danry, Javier Hernandez, Andrew Wilson, Pattie Maes, Judith Amores
